function.name: generate_image
function.description: Generate a NEW image from scratch based on a text description. Use this only for a new image, not for modifying an existing image.

prompt.description: A detailed description of the image to generate. Keep content details such as monitor/screen resolution in the prompt; do not move them into size.

size.description: Optional output canvas size as WIDTHxHEIGHT pixels. Use only when the user explicitly asks for output image dimensions; omit for content details like a monitor or pixel-art resolution. Dimensions must be multiples of 64. Otherwise omit to use the {image_size} default.

width.description: Optional output width in pixels. Use with height when dimensions are provided separately.

height.description: Optional output height in pixels. Use with width when dimensions are provided separately.

steps.description: Optional sampling/denoising step count. Use only when the user asks for speed, quality, detail, or a specific step count.

cfg_scale.description: Optional text guidance scale. Higher values follow the prompt more strongly; lower values allow more variation. Use only when requested or clearly implied.

seed.description: Optional random seed for reproducible generation. Use when the user provides a seed or asks for reproducibility.

sample_method.description: Optional sampler name/method. Use only when the user explicitly requests a sampler.

flow_shift.description: Optional flow shift value for models/backends that support it. Use only when requested or model guidance requires it.

tools.prompt_guidance: IMPORTANT: When an image has already been generated in this conversation and the user wants to add something, remove something, change, modify, or adjust the image in any way, you MUST use the edit_image tool — NOT generate_image. Only use generate_image for creating a brand new image from scratch. The edit_image tool automatically uses the most recent image as its source.

function.name: edit_image
function.description: Edit or modify the most recent generated image from this conversation. Use this for requested changes to an existing image, not for creating a brand new image.

prompt.description: A description of the desired edit or modification. Keep content details such as monitor/screen resolution in the prompt; do not move them into size.

size.description: Optional output canvas size as WIDTHxHEIGHT pixels. Use only when the user explicitly asks for output image dimensions; omit for content details like a monitor or pixel-art resolution. Dimensions must be multiples of 64. Otherwise omit to use the {image_size} default.

width.description: Optional output width in pixels. Use with height when dimensions are provided separately.

height.description: Optional output height in pixels. Use with width when dimensions are provided separately.

steps.description: Optional sampling/denoising step count. Use only when the user asks for speed, quality, detail, or a specific step count.

cfg_scale.description: Optional text guidance scale. Higher values follow the prompt more strongly; lower values allow more variation. Use only when requested or clearly implied.

seed.description: Optional random seed for reproducible generation. Use when the user provides a seed or asks for reproducibility.

sample_method.description: Optional sampler name/method. Use only when the user explicitly requests a sampler.

flow_shift.description: Optional flow shift value for models/backends that support it. Use only when requested or model guidance requires it.

function.name: text_to_speech
function.description: Convert text to spoken audio. Use this when the user asks you to speak, say, read aloud, or convert text to speech.

input.description: The text to convert to speech

voice.description: Voice to use for speech synthesis

tools.prompt_guidance: When you see '[User provided audio file #N]' in a message, it means the user sent audio data. Call the transcribe_audio tool to transcribe it — the audio data is handled automatically by the system.

function.name: transcribe_audio
function.description: Transcribe audio to text (speech-to-text). Use this when the user provides an audio file or when you see '[User provided audio file #N]' placeholders in the conversation. The audio data is automatically provided by the system — just call this tool with the language parameter.

language.description: Language of the audio (ISO 639-1 code, e.g. 'en', 'es', 'fr')

tools.prompt_guidance: When the user sends an image (as an image_url in their message), use the analyze_image tool to look at the image before responding about it.

function.name: analyze_image
function.description: Analyze, describe, or answer questions about an image. Use this when the user shares an image and asks you to look at it, describe it, read text from it, identify objects, or answer any question about what's in the image.

image_url.description: The URL or base64 data URI of the image to analyze

question.description: The question to answer about the image, or 'describe' for a general description
